title: Lindsey Graham, longtime senator from South Carolina, dies at 71
description: Graham, first elected to the House in 1994 and the Senate in 2002, was running for reelection this year. He died of a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said.

title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

title: Week in Politics: Trump's housing bill protest fails; Trump and NATO; Maine's Democrats
description: President Trump refused to sign a housing bill, now law, in protest over Congress not passing new restrictions on voting.

title: Inside the GOP playbook: Republican strategist Liam Donovan on reclaiming the majority
description: NPR's Scott Simon asks Republican strategist Liam Donovan about his party's approach to November's Senate races.

title: Political scientist Dominic Tierney on why the U.S. gets trapped in conflicts like Iran
description: NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Swarthmore College political science professor Dominic Tierney about the U.S.-Iran war and other conflicts that have left the U.S. in drawn-out entanglements.

title: Minnesota is pulling troops early from D.C., as pressure grows on Michigan
description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is pulling his National Guard early from Washington, D.C. as the chorus against Democratic governors sending troops to the city amid President Trump's ongoing deployment grows louder.

title: Graham Platner makes it official in Maine, submitting paperwork to leave Senate race
description: In his withdrawal notice, Platner said "people are desperate for change" and that's why they made him the Democratic nominee. Now, Maine Democrats have to pick someone to replace him by July 27.

title: Do scandals still matter in politics?
description: Scandals have always been a part of politics but the way politicians handle them has changed.

title: Largest housing affordability bill in decades becomes law without Trump's signature
description: President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.

title: One U.S. visa program is growing rapidly. No one is happy with it
description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are starting to talk about one facet of immigration reform: how to expand the popular H-2A visa program for farm laborers. They face obstacles.

title: Trump leans on 'communist' messaging as economic angst drives Democrats
description: During the last election, when struggling to find a memorable attack line against Kamala Harris, Trump eventually landed on "Comrade Kamala."
